title: pedicularis elwesii var. canescens
intro: Pedicularis elwesii var. canescens, also known as Himalayan lousewort, is a species of flowering plant in the bumblebee-pollinated genus Pedicularis. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pedicularis elwesii var. canescens include reducing inflammation, relieving symptoms of bronchitis and asthma, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
